questions.q: The general electronic configuration of s-block elements is?
questions.explanation: The s-block elements have a general electronic configuration of ns^1 or ns^2, where n is the outermost energy level.

questions.q: Which of the following s-block elements is the most reactive?
questions.explanation: Lithium (Li) is the most reactive among the s-block elements due to its small atomic size and high electronegativity.

questions.q: The valency of s-block elements is?
questions.explanation: The valency of s-block elements is 1, as they tend to lose one electron to achieve a stable noble gas configuration.

questions.q: Which s-block element is used as a reducing agent in the extraction of metals?
questions.explanation: Calcium (Ca) is used as a reducing agent in the extraction of metals, such as aluminum and magnesium.

questions.q: What is the primary source of sodium in the Earth's crust?
questions.explanation: Sodium chloride (NaCl) deposits, also known as halite, are the primary source of sodium in the Earth's crust.

questions.q: Which s-block element is used in the manufacture of soaps and detergents?
questions.explanation: Sodium (Na) is used in the manufacture of soaps and detergents, as it is an effective cleaning agent.

questions.q: The ionization enthalpy of s-block elements decreases as we move down the group?
questions.explanation: The ionization enthalpy of s-block elements decreases as we move down the group, due to the decrease in atomic size and increase in the distance between the nucleus and the valence electrons.

questions.q: Which of the following s-block elements is used in the manufacture of matchsticks?
questions.explanation: Potassium (K) is used in the manufacture of matchsticks, as it is a highly reactive metal that ignites easily.

questions.q: The reactivity of s-block elements decreases as we move down the group?
questions.explanation: The reactivity of s-block elements increases as we move down the group, due to the increase in atomic size and decrease in electronegativity.

questions.q: What is the primary use of magnesium (Mg) in the chemical industry?
opts: (D) As a reactant in the manufacture of soaps
questions.explanation: Magnesium (Mg) is primarily used as a reducing agent in the chemical industry, particularly in the extraction of metals such as titanium and zirconium.

questions.q: Which s-block element is used in the manufacture of glass?
questions.explanation: Sodium (Na) is used in the manufacture of glass, as it lowers the melting point of silica and makes it easier to melt.

questions.q: The s-block elements are known for their high reactivity due to their?
questions.explanation: The s-block elements are known for their high reactivity due to their small atomic size, which allows them to easily lose electrons to form positive ions.

questions.q: Which s-block element is used in the manufacture of dry cells?
questions.explanation: Sodium (Na) is used in the manufacture of dry cells, as it provides a high voltage and long shelf life.

questions.q: The ionization enthalpy of s-block elements is influenced by the?
opts: (C) Distance between the nucleus and the valence electrons
questions.explanation: The ionization enthalpy of s-block elements is influenced by all of the above factors, including the nuclear charge, electron-electron repulsions, and distance between the nucleus and the valence electrons.

questions.q: Which s-block element is used in the manufacture of fireworks?
questions.explanation: Potassium (K) is used in the manufacture of fireworks, as it produces a bright red or orange color when heated.

questions.q: The s-block elements exhibit a range of oxidation states due to the?
questions.explanation: The s-block elements exhibit a range of oxidation states due to the presence of multiple valence electrons, which can be lost or gained to form different ions.

questions.q: Which s-block element is used in the manufacture of semiconductors?
questions.explanation: Silicon (Si) is used in the manufacture of semiconductors, as it has a high resistance to electrical current and can be easily doped to change its conductivity.

questions.q: The s-block elements are highly reactive due to the?
opts: (A) Presence of lone pairs on the valence electrons
(B) Presence of multiple bonds between atoms
(C) Presence of a full outer energy level
(D) Presence of a half-full outer energy level
questions.explanation: The s-block elements are highly reactive due to the presence of a half-full outer energy level, which makes them easily lose electrons to form positive ions.

questions.q: Which s-block element is used in the manufacture of solar panels?
questions.explanation: Cadmium (Cd) is used in the manufacture of solar panels, as it has a high absorption coefficient and can be easily alloyed with other metals to create a conductive material.
